name: TitleBucks Title Loans
description_short: Vehicle title lender offering secured loans up to $25,000 using your car title as collateral. No perfect credit required; funds available in as little as 30 minutes in-store.
description_long:
TitleBucks was founded in 1987 and operates under TMX Finance, a subsidiary of Community Choice Financial (CCF Holdings), headquartered in Thomasville, Georgia. The company runs approximately 200 branded store locations across states including Alabama, Arizona, Georgia, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas. TitleBucks holds no CDFI, HUD-approved, or NFCC designations — it is a for-profit licensed lender regulated in each state it operates. It maintains an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau across multiple store-level profiles.

TitleBucks's core offering is the vehicle title loan — a short-term secured loan where the customer's vehicle title serves as collateral. Loan amounts run up to $10,000, with qualified customers in select markets eligible for up to $25,000; typical funded amounts range from $1,000 to $3,000 based on vehicle value and loan requested. No perfect credit is required. In Georgia, the same product is called a title pawn due to state-specific regulations. Beyond title loans, TitleBucks also offers personal installment loans, online personal loans, and signature loans (unsecured). In Texas, the company operates as a Credit Access Business (CAB), meaning loans are originated through a third-party lender. Applicants must be 18 or older (19 in Alabama), hold a qualifying vehicle title, and not be active-duty military or a military dependent.

TitleBucks distinguishes itself within the title lending industry primarily through speed — approved borrowers can receive funds in as little as 30 minutes in-store. Customers retain use of their vehicle throughout the loan term, a practical advantage over traditional pawn arrangements where collateral is surrendered. The company's nearly four-decade operational history and multi-state footprint under Community Choice Financial offer institutional stability. Online tools including a 24/7 payment portal at titlebucks.com and the TitleMax Mobile App (shared across the CCF brand family) allow payment management without requiring in-store visits.

TitleBucks fills a real market gap for credit-challenged consumers who need fast, asset-backed access to cash. That said, the company does not publicly disclose APRs or interest rates — a meaningful transparency gap that requires borrowers to visit a store or complete an application before learning the actual cost of borrowing. Title loans as a product category are associated with high interest rates and rollover risk; borrowers who cannot repay face vehicle repossession. Geographic coverage is limited to roughly six states. For any consumer with access to a credit union, CDFI, or lower-APR personal loan alternative, those options should be evaluated before choosing a title loan.
address: 1529 W St Marys Rd, Tucson, AZ 85745

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee found. Title loans are lending products with no returns policy.

pros: Funds disbursed in as little as 30 minutes in-store after approval
No perfect credit required — accepts most credit histories
Customers keep driving their vehicle throughout the full loan repayment period
A+ BBB rating across multiple store-level profiles
Founded in 1987 — nearly 40 years of vehicle title lending experience
Loan amounts up to $25,000 for qualified customers in select markets
24/7 online payment portal and mobile app available for account management
cons: APR and interest rates not publicly disclosed — must visit a store or apply to learn actual borrowing cost
Active-duty military service members and their dependents are ineligible by federal law
Vehicle repossession is a real risk if the borrower defaults on repayment
Available in approximately six states only — limited geographic coverage
Short-term, high-cost product structure is not suitable as a long-term financing solution
best_for: Credit-challenged consumers who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Vehicle owners in eligible states needing $1,000–$10,000 quickly
Individuals who need same-day cash access without surrendering their vehicle
Borrowers who have exhausted lower-cost lending options and own a vehicle outright or with equity
diagnosis: TitleBucks is best suited for credit-challenged consumers in its roughly six operating states who own a vehicle and need rapid access to $1,000–$10,000 in cash. The central caveat is that interest rates are not publicly disclosed and title loans as a category carry high borrowing costs — plus the real consequence of vehicle repossession on default — making this a last-resort option rather than a first choice for most borrowers.
services: Vehicle title loans secured by car title
Title pawns (Georgia-specific equivalent of title loans)
In-store loan servicing and account management
24/7 online loan payment portal at titlebucks.com
Mobile app payment management via TitleMax Mobile App
Credit Access Business (CAB) loan facilitation in Texas
